element_introduction_order.mastery_context: Players learn timing and gap identification in safe, predictable lanes before encountering river hazards
Introduces platform riding and directional awareness; complexity increases with moving obstacles in opposite directions

element_introduction_order.element: Snakes and otters (passive hazards in river)
element_introduction_order.mastery_context: Collision avoidance on moving platforms; requires spatial prediction
Visual recognition of threat state; timing-dependent avoidance
Introduces time-based platform reliability; requires forward planning
Optional risk-reward mechanic; teaches players to recognize bonuses amid threats
Optional precision bonus; rewards accurate targeting
Reduces available safe destinations; forces route optimization
Home deception; requires careful home selection under time pressure

typical_relief_triggers: Successfully reaching a safe median strip
Completing a full level (all 5 frogs home)
typical_tension_triggers: Spawning at bottom with active traffic
Jumping onto moving river platforms in opposite directions
Proximity to screen edge while riding platforms
rhythm_pattern.pacing_descriptor: Cyclical tension within single-frog attempts (30-second timer creating micro-tension cycles), interrupted by relief upon reaching median or home; macro-tension escalates across five-level progression curve then resets

spatial_grammar.optional_content_signal: Female frog appears randomly on logs; fly bonus appears in homes; both offer bonuses without blocking safe progression
spatial_grammar.secret_convention: Homes with alligators appear identical to safe homes; requires learning through failure or careful observation

difficulty_curve.early_game: Road traffic with basic predictable patterns; river introduces moving obstacles but at learnable speeds; timer at 30-60 seconds per frog allows adjustment
difficulty_curve.mid_game: Traffic speed increases; river objects move at varied speeds in opposing directions; available homes reduce as they become occupied by alligators; timer pressure increases
difficulty_curve.late_game: Maximal traffic density and speed; river obstacles move unpredictably; false safe destinations (alligators in homes) force routing precision; minimal timer margin
notable_spikes: Transition from road to river (introduces platform-dependent safety)
Level 5-6 transition (difficulty briefly eases then escalates again)
Homes becoming occupied with alligators (converts safe zone into threat)

notable_level_patterns.description: Road section consists of horizontal lanes with predictable vehicle patterns; players identify gaps and timing windows
River contains logs and creatures moving left and right simultaneously; requires path-finding through moving obstacles at different speeds
Available safe homes decrease as game progresses (alligators occupy slots, other frogs already home); forces player to use remaining safe routes with increasing pressure
Diving turtles sink on schedule; players must time platform usage or face unexpected loss
